options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: death_link_heart
options.description: Whenever DeathLinks are received they will be nullified at the cost of a heart

options.name: damage_link
options.description: Damage is shared across slots

options.name: trap_link
options.description: Whether your received traps are linked to other players

options.name: energy_link
options.description:
Allows players to contribute to the EnergyLink pool by collecting coins

Players can exchange EnergyLink coins for consumable items

options.name: early_climb
options.description:
Force Climb to appear early in the seed as a local item.

This is particularly useful to prevent BK when Level Shuffle is disabled

options.name: goal
options.description:
Determines the goal of the seed

Bowser: Find a certain number of Yoshi Eggs, reach Bowser's Castle and defeat Bowser

Yoshi's House: Find a certain number of Yoshi Eggs and bring them back to Yoshi's House

options.name: yoshi_egg_count
options.description:
Amount of Golden Yoshi Eggs that can be found anywhere in the multiworld/itempool.

The amount of eggs rolled by this option will be added to the amount of eggs created by the Local Golden Yoshi Eggs option
and both of them will not exceed the 255 egg limit.

options.name: percentage_of_yoshi_eggs
options.display_name: Required Percentage of Golden Yoshi Eggs
options.description:
What Percentage of Yoshi Eggs are required to finish.

This option will sum yoshi_egg_placement and yoshi_egg_count, then it'll calculate the required amount from that egg count.

options.name: yoshi_egg_placement
options.description:
Forces Golden Yoshi Eggs into specific level exits.
This option will create a new location in each level that's valid, that way it doesn't disturb your potential Priority Locations
in said regions. Similar to how Boss Tokens operated before.

The "Every Level" option has priority over the others, creating an at least 96 egg game.

The amount of eggs rolled by this option will be added to the amount of eggs created by the yoshi_egg_count option
and both of them will not exceed the 255 egg limit.

options.name: ability_shuffle
options.description:
Which abilities and items will be added as items in the item pool
If an ability is not present in the list they will be treated as unlocked from the start

options.name: game_logic_difficulty
alternate_logic
options.description: Alternate logic for certain locations.
options: Cheese Bridge Area - Secret Exit with Yoshi
Forest Ghost House - Skip second room
Forest of Illusion 1 - Secret Exit with Yoshi
Forest of Illusion 3 - Can pass big pipe itemless
Forest of Illusion 3 - Secret Exit with Yoshi
Star World 3 - Top area with a Star
Star World 4 - Carryless exit with wingless Yoshi
Valley Ghost House - True Carryless Secret Exit
Valley of Bowser 3 - Itemless Powerup block
Vanilla Dome 4 - Sacrifice for Coin Block #8

options.name: inventory_yoshi_logic
options.description:
Whether being able to use Yoshi inventory items is considered in logic or not for Yoshi related logic
If disabled, you need to reach a level with a Yoshi block on it in order to have Yoshi related checks in logic.

options.name: decoupled_yoshi_carry
options.description:
Whether Yoshi's Tongue requires an additional Progressive Yoshi item to be unlocked or not.
If disabled, Yoshi will be able to use his tongue immediately after unlocking him.

options.name: decoupled_wall_run_anywhere
options.description:
Whether Wall Run Anywhere requires an additional Progressive Run item or not.
If disabled, Mario will gain the ability right after getting a Run item.

options.name: decoupled_fast_swimming
options.description:
Whether Fast Swimming requires an additional Progressive Swim item or not.
If disabled, Mario will gain the ability right after getting a Swim item.

options.name: luigi_physics
options.description:
Enables SMA2 Luigi physics.
WARNING: Logic does not account for those physics and probably never will.
If you're watching this you're very likely a power user or your front end is revealing a bit too much info lol.

options.name: dragon_coin_checks
options.description: Whether collecting 5 Dragon Coins in each level will grant a check

options.name: moon_checks
options.description: Whether collecting a 3-Up Moon in a level will grant a check

options.name: hidden_1up_checks
options.description:
Whether collecting a hidden 1-Up mushroom in a level will grant a check

These checks are considered cryptic as there's no actual indicator that they're in their respective places

Enable this option at your own risk

options.name: star_block_checks
options.description: Whether collecting a prize from a Star Block in a level will grant a check

options.name: midway_point_checks
options.description: Whether collecting a Midway Point in a level will grant a check

options.name: room_checks
options.description: Whether visiting a room in levels will grant a check

options.name: block_checks
options.description:
Whether hitting a block with an item or coin inside will grant a check

Note that some blocks are excluded due to how the option and the game works!
Exclusion list:
  * Blocks in Top Secret Area & Front Door/Bowser Castle
  * Blocks that are unreachable unless you glitch your way in

options.name: bowser_castle_doors
options.description:
How the doors of Bowser's Castle behave

Vanilla: Front and Back Doors behave as vanilla

Fast: Both doors behave as the Back Door

Slow: Both doors behave as the Front Door

"Front Door" rooms depend on the `bowser_castle_rooms` option

"Back Door" only requires going through the dark hallway to Bowser

options.name: bowser_castle_rooms
options.description:
How the rooms of Bowser's Castle Front Door behave

Vanilla: You can choose which rooms to enter, as in vanilla

Random Two Room: Two random rooms are chosen

Random Five Room: Five random rooms are chosen

Gauntlet: All eight rooms must be cleared

Labyrinth: Which room leads to Bowser?

options.name: level_effects
options.description:
How many levels will receive a permanent random trap effect in them
Which traps are going to be used is going to be determined by their trap weight

Valid traps: Ice, Reverse, Screen Flip, Pixelate, Spotlight, Invisibility

Setting Game Logic Difficulty option to easy or medium is recommended.

options.name: level_shuffle
options.description: Whether levels are shuffled

options.name: starting_location
options.description:
Which location is your starting one
May interact incorrectly with forced Collects
Will throw errors on certain option combinations, please test your YAML

options.name: map_teleport_shuffle
options.description: Whether map teleports (stars and pipes) are shuffled

options.name: map_transition_shuffle
options.description: Wheter map transitions are shuffled

options.name: carryless_exits
options.description: Swaps some Keyholes with a Red Goal Orb which triggers the Secret Exit upon touching it

options.name: swap_exit_count
options.description:
How many levels will have their exits swapped

Star World 5 and Donut Secret House are excluded from this feature

options.name: swap_donut_gh_exits
options.description:
If enabled, this option will swap which overworld direction the two exits of the level at the Donut Ghost House overworld tile go:

False: Normal Exit goes up, Secret Exit goes right.

True: Normal Exit goes right, Secret Exit goes up.

This option has priority over Swap Level Exits.

options.name: boss_shuffle
options.description:
How bosses are shuffled

None: Bosses are not shuffled

Simple: Four Reznors and the seven Koopalings are shuffled around

Full: Each boss location gets a fully random boss

Singularity: One or two bosses are chosen and placed at every boss location

options.name: enemy_shuffle
options.description: Shuffles around enemies.

options.name: junk_fill_percentage
options.description: Replace a percentage of non-required Yoshi Eggs in the item pool with random junk items

options.name: trap_fill_percentage
options.description: Replace a percentage of junk items in the item pool with random traps

options.name: ice_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes the level to become slippery

options.name: stun_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which briefly stuns Mario

options.name: literature_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes the player to read literature

options.name: timer_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes the timer to run low

options.name: reverse_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes the controls to be reversed in the current level

options.name: thwimp_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes a Thwimp to spawn above the player

options.name: fishin_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes a Fishin' Boo to spawn above the player

options.name: screen_flip_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes the screen to flip vertically

options.name: sticky_floor_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes the floor become sticky

options.name: sticky_hands_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes the player's hands become sticky

options.name: pixelate_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes a the entire screen become pixelated

options.name: spotlight_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes a spotlight to appear on the player's position

options.name: bullet_time_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which slows down the game

options.name: invisibility_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which makes the player invisible

options.name: empty_item_box_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which removes the item in the item box

options.name: display_received_item_popups
options.description: What messages to display in-game for items received

options.name: music_shuffle
options.description:
Music shuffle type

None: No Music is shuffled

Consistent: Each music track is consistently shuffled throughout the game

Full: Each individual level has a random music track

Singularity: The entire game uses one song for overworld and one song for levels

options.name: sfx_shuffle
options.description:
Shuffles almost every instance of sound effect playback

Archipelago elements that play sound effects aren't randomized

None: No SFX are shuffled

Full: Each individual SFX call has a random SFX

Singularity: The entire game uses one SFX for every SFX call

options.name: mario_palette
options.description: Mario palette color

options.name: ungolden_eggs
options.description:
Does exactly what you're thinking of.
(Removes Useful Tag from eggs and nothing else)
If you're watching this you're very likely a power user or your front end is revealing a bit too much info lol.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
